title: No Contagion, Only Interdependence: Measuring Stock Market Comovements
one_line_relevance: Shows unconditional correlations barely rise in crises once you correct for heteroskedasticity — the foundational 'spurious contagion' result.

title: Extreme Correlation of International Equity Markets
one_line_relevance: Cross-country equity correlations rise in bear-market tails but not bull tails — asymmetric regime behaviour via extreme-value theory.

title: Dynamic Conditional Correlation: A Simple Class of Multivariate GARCH Models
one_line_relevance: Introduces DCC-GARCH — the standard parametric model for time-varying correlation used by essentially every safe-haven paper since.

authors: Baur, D. G.; Lucey, B. M.
title: Is Gold a Hedge or a Safe Haven? An Analysis of Stocks, Bonds and Gold
one_line_relevance: Canonical hedge-vs-safe-haven distinction for gold; empirically gold is a hedge on average and a safe haven in extremes for ~15 trading days.

authors: Baur, D. G.; McDermott, T. K.
title: Is Gold a Safe Haven? International Evidence
one_line_relevance: Extends Baur-Lucey across 30 years and many countries; gold is safe haven for developed European / US equities but not BRICs or AU/CA/JP.

title: Is gold a safe haven or a hedge for the US dollar? Implications for risk management
one_line_relevance: Copula analysis: gold hedges USD depreciation on average and is a tail-symmetric safe haven against extreme dollar moves.

authors: Bredin, D.; Conlon, T.; Potì, V.
title: Does Gold Glitter in the Long-Run? Gold as a Hedge and Safe Haven Across Time and Investment Horizon
one_line_relevance: Wavelet decomposition shows gold is a safe haven across ALL horizons during crises but downside-protective only short-horizon in recessions.

title: International Asset Allocation With Regime Shifts
one_line_relevance: Regime-switching model where correlations + vols rise together in bear regimes; methodological template for conditioning correlations on macro state.

title: A New Approach to the Economic Analysis of Nonstationary Time Series and the Business Cycle
one_line_relevance: Markov regime-switching framework — will be used to identify macroeconomic regimes within which to condition our correlations.

title: Selection of estimation window in the presence of breaks
one_line_relevance: Shows that pre-break data can improve out-of-sample forecasts even across structural breaks — informs our choice of rolling-correlation window length.

title: Bitcoin, gold and the dollar – A GARCH volatility analysis
one_line_relevance: Early BTC paper: initial GARCH evidence that bitcoin sits between gold and USD as hedge — a claim that 2020+ data would overturn.

authors: Bouri, E.; Molnár, P.; Azzi, G.; Roubaud, D.; Hagfors, L. I.
title: On the hedge and safe haven properties of Bitcoin: Is it really more than a diversifier?
one_line_relevance: Seminal bitcoin paper: BTC is mostly a diversifier, only a weak/conditional safe haven against Asian equities.

authors: Shahzad, S. J. H.; Bouri, E.; Roubaud, D.; Kristoufek, L.; Lucey, B. M.
title: Is Bitcoin a better safe-haven investment than gold and commodities?
one_line_relevance: Cross-quantilogram weak/strong safe-haven definition; BTC is only weak SH, role is time-varying and index-dependent.

title: Bitcoin as a safe haven: Is it even worth considering?
one_line_relevance: Argues BTC's volatility and liquidity make it unsuitable as safe haven; a weak hedge at best.

title: Safe haven or risky hazard? Bitcoin during the Covid-19 bear market
one_line_relevance: March 2020: BTC fell alongside S&P 500 and INCREASED portfolio downside risk — direct falsification of the digital-gold claim under stress.

authors: Akhtaruzzaman, M.; Boubaker, S.; Lucey, B. M.; Sensoy, A.
title: Is gold a hedge or a safe-haven asset in the COVID–19 crisis?
one_line_relevance: High-frequency DCC analysis during COVID finds gold's safe-haven role weakened in early pandemic period.

authors: Selmi, R.; Mensi, W.; Hammoudeh, S.; Bouoiyour, J.
title: Is Bitcoin a hedge, a safe haven or a diversifier for oil price movements? A comparison with gold
one_line_relevance: Quantile-on-quantile shows safe-haven role of BTC/gold is conditional on market state (bear/normal/bull).

title: Speculative bubbles in Bitcoin markets? An empirical investigation into the fundamental value of Bitcoin
one_line_relevance: Argues BTC fundamental value is zero and prices contain large bubble component — relevant to whether BTC correlations are behaviourally driven.

title: Facts and Fantasies About Commodity Futures
one_line_relevance: Historical (1959-2004): collateralised commodity futures earned equity-like returns and were NEGATIVELY correlated with equities and bonds.

authors: Erb, C. B.; Harvey, C. R.
title: The Strategic and Tactical Value of Commodity Futures
one_line_relevance: Companion to Gorton-Rouwenhorst — individual commodities near-zero excess return but rebalanced portfolios get an equity-like premium.

authors: Driesprong, G.; Jacobsen, B.; Maat, B.
title: Striking oil: Another puzzle?
one_line_relevance: Oil price changes statistically predict global stock returns in 12 of 18 countries — oil-equity link is nontrivial.

title: Index Investment and Financialization of Commodities
one_line_relevance: Post-2004 financialisation: non-energy commodity futures became increasingly correlated with oil and with equities, especially for index-member commodities.

title: Financialization of Commodity Markets
one_line_relevance: Review of financialisation literature; post-2008 commodity-equity correlation jumped from near zero to ~+0.5 and stayed elevated.

title: The impact of monetary policy on asset prices
one_line_relevance: Heteroskedasticity-based identification: tighter policy moves stocks down and the yield curve up — baseline for Fed-regime conditioning.

authors: Forbes, K. J.; Warnock, F. E.
title: Capital flow waves: Surges, stops, flight, and retrenchment
one_line_relevance: Global risk drives extreme capital-flow episodes; the global financial cycle is a candidate driver of cross-asset correlation regimes.

authors: Campbell, J. Y.; Pflueger, C.; Viceira, L. M.
title: Macroeconomic Drivers of Bond and Equity Risks
one_line_relevance: Sign of inflation-output-gap correlation flips bond-stock correlation — same mechanism likely drives gold-stock correlation changes.

authors: Campbell, J. Y.; Sunderam, A.; Viceira, L. M.
title: Inflation Bets or Deflation Hedges? The Changing Risks of Nominal Bonds
one_line_relevance: Foundational paper showing bond-stock covariance moves from positive (pre-2000) to negative (2000s) due to changing inflation risk.

authors: Baker, S. R.; Bloom, N.; Davis, S. J.
title: Measuring Economic Policy Uncertainty
one_line_relevance: EPU index — candidate macro regime variable for conditioning cross-asset correlations.

title: Measuring Geopolitical Risk
one_line_relevance: Canonical GPR index (1985-present, 10-newspaper) — primary candidate for our geopolitical-regime conditioning variable.

title: Does gold hedge stock market, inflation and exchange rate risks? An econometric investigation
one_line_relevance: Gold hedges inflation in US only in average/bearish gold regimes; not a universal inflation hedge.

title: Gold as an Inflation Hedge in a Time-Varying Coefficient Framework
venue: North American Journal of Economics and Finance 24 208-222
one_line_relevance: Gold's inflation-hedging coefficient is regime-dependent and varies across the USA/UK/JP/EU — evidence against a constant-correlation view.

title: Liquidity and Leverage
one_line_relevance: Marked-to-market leverage is procyclical; forced deleveraging connects liquidity crises to simultaneous cross-asset drawdowns.

title: Expected Returns: An Investor's Guide to Harvesting Market Rewards
one_line_relevance: Textbook: macro factors (growth, inflation, liquidity, tail) drive cross-asset correlation; correlations vary with regime.

title: The Fiscal Theory of the Price Level
one_line_relevance: Fiscal theory of the price level: inflation is pinned down by the present value of surpluses — intellectual basis for the 'debasement trade' narrative.

title: Fiscal Policy, Monetary Policy and Central Bank Independence
venue: Jackson Hole Symposium (Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City)
one_line_relevance: Argues ZLB-era fiscal-dominance: monetary policy is effectively constrained by fiscal stance; complements the debasement-trade reasoning.

authors: Bouri, E.; Shahzad, S. J. H.; Roubaud, D.; Kristoufek, L.; Lucey, B. M.
title: Bitcoin, gold, and commodities as safe havens for stocks: New insight through wavelet analysis
venue: Quarterly Review of Economics and Finance 77 156-164
one_line_relevance: Wavelet evidence that the safe-haven role of BTC/gold/commodities is frequency-dependent and largely absent at high frequencies.

title: Cryptocurrency as an alternative inflation hedge?
one_line_relevance: Post-2020 re-examination finds bitcoin is NOT an effective inflation hedge — contradicts 'digital gold' narrative.

title: Is bitcoin an inflation hedge?
one_line_relevance: Most recent published empirical: BTC does not systematically hedge inflation shocks; its return is dominated by risk-on/off flows.

authors: World Gold Council (Cavatoni, J.; Reade, J.)
title: Why bitcoin isn't the new gold
one_line_relevance: Industry-body decomposition of gold drivers (real rates, DXY, geopolitics) vs BTC drivers (risk flows, ETF adoption).

authors: Caldara, D.; Conlisk, S.; Iacoviello, M.; Penn, M.
title: Do Geopolitical Risks Raise or Lower Inflation?
venue: Federal Reserve Board manuscript (forthcoming J. Monetary Econ.)
one_line_relevance: Shows GPR shocks raise inflation and depress output in importing countries — supports a stagflationary-regime conditioning channel.

authors: Bank for International Settlements (Rees, D. M.)
title: Commodity prices and the US dollar
one_line_relevance: Documents the 2021-2023 breakdown of the historical inverse commodity-USD correlation — commodities and USD now co-move.

title: Commodity prices, the dollar and stagflation risk
one_line_relevance: Joint USD+commodity spike in 2021-22 created a stagflationary regime — earlier, the two usually offset.

title: The Global Dollar Cycle
one_line_relevance: Global dollar cycle is a dominant driver of EM asset returns; candidate conditioning regime variable for correlation analysis.

title: Correlation in returns of bitcoin, gold, stocks and government bonds
one_line_relevance: 2024-2025 rolling-correlation panel showing BTC strongly correlated with S&P 500 while gold remains weakly negative — the stylised fact we want to verify.

title: Gold and Bitcoin Decouple. What's Driving the Divergence?
one_line_relevance: Industry analysis of the 2025 gold/BTC decoupling — BTC joined the equity block while gold strengthened the debasement trade.
